---
title: "Diana M. Ochoa-Sanz"
toc: false
---

::: {.profile-header}
::: {.profile-left}
![](../images/equipo/diana.jpeg){.profile-photo-large}

::: {.profile-links}
[✉](mailto:diana.ochoa@posgrado.ecologia.edu.mx)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-right}
**PhD Student**  
Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (INECOL)

*Interests:* [Bats]{.profile-tag} [Trophic Ecology]{.profile-tag} [Macroevolution]{.profile-tag}

I am a biology graduate from Pontificia Universidad Javeriana Cali, Colombia, and I obtained my master's degree at INECOL. Since the beginning of my career, I have been fascinated by the ecological and evolutionary patterns shaping the diversity of phyllostomid bats, with a special interest in their extraordinary trophic diversity. My doctoral research focuses on understanding the dimensions of dietary specialization and their relationship with rarity patterns in phyllostomids.

### Education

- **PhD** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (ongoing)
- **MSc** — Instituto de Ecología, A.C. (2024)
- **BSc** — Pontificia Universidad Javeriana Cali, Colombia (2021)
:::
:::

::: {.profile-back}
[← Back to Team](../equipo.html)
:::
